perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will use the Information only for the purpose of collaboration or trade with the Disclosing Party and shall not otherwise use or exploit any of the information for its own or any other person’s gain, benefit or purpose and, in particul ar but without limitation, it will not use any of the Information for commercial or competitive purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will use the Information freely, including exploiting the information for its own or any other person’s gain, benefit or purpose including for commercial or competitive purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This directly contradicts the original clause, which restricted the use of information. Now, the recipient is explicitly allowed to use the information for any purpose, including commercial gain and competitive advantage. This makes the entire agreement self-defeating, as the purpose of an NDA is to protect information, not allow its free use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient recognises and acknowledges the Disclosing Party’s representation of the competitive value and confidential nature of the Information and Materials and that any use or 4 disclosure of the Information or Materials o ther than as expressly authorised by this agreement will be detrimental to the Disclosing Party, and may result in the Disclosing Party suffering substantial and irreparable loss and damage. Without affecting any other rights or remedies that any party may have, the Recipient acknowledges and agrees that due to the character of the Information to be made available hereunder, damages would not be an adequate remedy for any breach by it or any of its directors, officers, employees, agents or advisers of the provisions of this agreement, and that the Disclosing Party will be entitled to the remedies of injunction, specific performance and other equitable relief for any threatened or actual breach of the provisions of this agreement by the Recipient or any of i ts directors, officers, employees, agents or advisers and that no proof of special damage or special damages shall be necessary for the enforcement of this agreement. Nothing contained in this paragraph shall, however, be construed as a waiver by the Disc losing Party of any other rights, including without limitation, rights for damages.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and contradicted text both aim to protect the Disclosing Party's confidential information and restrict the Recipient's use of it to collaboration or trade with the Disclosing Party. The changed text now permits the Recipient to freely use the information for any purpose, including commercial and competitive gain, which directly conflicts with the Disclosing Party's expectation of confidentiality and restricted use. This creates a clear and direct conflict and makes the entire agreement self-defeating, as it allows the recipient to exploit information that is meant to be protected.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will not (directly or indirectly) at any time, without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, disclose or reveal or permit access to the Information, in whole or in part, to any person other than its employees and agents with a need to know in connection with the performance of the collaboration or trade with the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may, at any time, disclose or reveal or permit access to the Information, in whole or in part, to any person without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: This change completely reverses the confidentiality obligation, allowing the Recipient to freely disclose information to anyone. This contradicts the core purpose of the NDA and makes the agreement unenforceable. This also contradicts section 5, that wants the information received at any time by it, safeguard as private an d confidential and take reasonable steps necessary or desirable to maintain the confidentiality and security of all Information received at any time by it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient will treat and safeguard as private an d confidential and take reasonable steps necessary or desirable to maintain the confidentiality and security of all Information received at any time by it.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Recipient may disclose information without the Disclosing Party's consent, directly contradicting the contradicted text, which requires the Recipient to safeguard information as private and confidential and take reasonable steps to maintain its confidentiality and security. The change fundamentally alters the NDA's purpose and creates an unenforceable agreement based on the perturbation explanation given.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will treat and safeguard as private an d confidential and take reasonable steps necessary or desirable to maintain the confidentiality and security of all Information received at any time by it.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient is not required to treat and safeguard as private an d confidential, nor to take reasonable steps necessary or desirable to maintain the confidentiality and security of all Information received at any time by it.
perturbation.explanation: This change explicitly states that the Recipient has no obligation to maintain confidentiality, negating a fundamental aspect of the NDA. It directly contradicts section 14, that says Recipient recognises and acknowledges the Disclosing Party’s representation of the competitive value and confidential nature of the Information and Materials.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient recognises and acknowledges the Disclosing Party’s representation of the competitive value and confidential nature of the Information and Materials and that any use or 4 disclosure of the Information or Materials o ther than as expressly authorised by this agreement will be detrimental to the Disclosing Party, and may result in the Disclosing Party suffering substantial and irreparable loss and damage. Without affecting any other rights or remedies that any party may have, the Recipient acknowledges and agrees that due to the character of the Information to be made available hereunder, damages would not be an adequate remedy for any breach by it or any of its directors, officers, employees, agents or advisers of the provisions of this agreement, and that the Disclosing Party will be entitled to the remedies of injunction, specific performance and other equitable relief for any threatened or actual breach of the provisions of this agreement by the Recipient or any of i ts directors, officers, employees, agents or advisers and that no proof of special damage or special damages shall be necessary for the enforcement of this agreement. Nothing contained in this paragraph shall, however, be construed as a waiver by the Disc losing Party of any other rights, including without limitation, rights for damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text obligates the recipient to safeguard confidential information. The changed text explicitly states that the Recipient is not required to treat and safeguard as private confidential information. This directly contradicts the section stating the Recipient recognizes the confidential nature of the Information and Materials, creating a direct and strong in-text contradiction. The explanation given also confirms that this change contradicts section 14.

perturbation.original_text: All of the Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and the disclosure of such Information and the Recipient access to the Materials shall not be deemed to confer upon the Recipien t any rights or licence whatsoever in respect of any part of the Information or Materials.
perturbation.changed_text: All of the Information shall become the property of the Recipient, and the disclosure of such Information and the Recipient access to the Materials shall be deemed to confer upon the Recipient all rights and licenses in respect of any part of the Information or Materials.
perturbation.explanation: This directly contradicts the original clause, by stating that all the information now belongs to the recipient. This also contradicts section 16, that states, the Disclosing Party retains sole ownership and title to the Information and Materials at all times. The Recipient shall k eep the Materials free of liens, attachments, and other encumbrances.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Disclosing Party retains sole ownership and title to the Information and Materials at all times. The Recipient shall k eep the Materials free of liens, attachments, and other encumbrances.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that all information shall become the property of the Recipient, granting them all rights and licenses. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that 'The Disclosing Party retains sole ownership and title to the Information and Materials at all times.' This creates a clear and direct conflict, as ownership cannot simultaneously reside with both parties. The original text stated the opposite of the changed text, and therefore did not cause the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Disclosing Party retains sole ownership and title to the Information and Materials at all times. The Recipient shall k eep the Materials free of liens, attachments, and other encumbrances.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient retains sole ownership and title to the Information and Materials at all times. The Disclosing party shall keep the materials free of liens, attachments, and other encumbrances.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly swaps the roles of who retains the ownership, and creates a contradiction in terms of who is in charge of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All of the Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and the disclosure of such Information and the Recipient access to the Materials shall not be deemed to confer upon the Recipien t any rights or licence whatsoever in respect of any part of the Information or Materials.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text directly contradicts the Contradicted Text. The Changed Text states 'The Recipient retains sole ownership and title to the Information and Materials at all times', while the Contradicted Text states 'All of the Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party'. This is a direct conflict regarding who owns the Information and Materials, therefore there is a strong in-text contradiction and is considered YES.
